decision-tree.root-question: Is at least one tool going to be reused by ≥2 agents or projects?

branch.conclusion: Skip MCP. Define the tool directly via LangChain / OpenAI tool-call SDK and ship in-process. No protocol overhead for a single-consumer tool.

branch.question: How many concurrent clients and where do they run?

branch.conclusion: Pick `stdio` transport. Set `auth.mode=none`. Scaffold from `templates/python-server-stdio.py`. Register via Claude Code `mcpServers.command`.
Skip MCP. Streamable HTTP round-trip alone is ~30-80 ms; the latency floor breaks the SLO. Embed the tool as in-process code or co-locate as a sidecar.
Pick `streamable-http` bound on `127.0.0.1` only. Set `auth.mode=none`. Acceptable for local multi-process fan-out; never bind on `0.0.0.0`.

Pick `streamable-http` with OAuth 2.1 Resource Server posture. Scaffold from `templates/python-server-streamable-http.py`. Advertise `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`. Set proxy idle timeout ≥ longest expected agent turn.

example.p: 1 client (Claude Code), localhost, 2 tools to be reused across upcoming logistics agents → q-need-mcp=yes → q-clients-and-location=1+localhost → conclusion r4-transport-choice (stdio).
